name: AI Security Hardening
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure AI Content Safety
services.purpose: Multi-category content moderation — hate, violence, self-harm, sexual, prompt injection detection

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for red team simulation, adversarial prompt generation, safety boundary testing

prod.note: ~1M tokens/day, automated red team sweeps + guardrails

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for continuous security testing

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Security proxy — content safety middleware, prompt shield enforcement, response filtering

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Security event store — blocked prompts, red team results, policy violations, audit log

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Content Safety keys, OpenAI API keys, mTLS certificates, encryption keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Security metrics — block rates, prompt injection attempts, red team coverage, false positives

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Security audit logs — all content safety decisions, red team sessions, policy changes

services.name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud
services.purpose: Cloud security posture — AI workload protection, threat detection, compliance scoring

enterprise.note: Full workload protection, runtime threat detection

optimization_tips: Use Content Safety free tier for dev — 5K transactions/month is sufficient for testing safety rules
Batch content safety checks — send prompt + response together in one API call instead of two separate calls
Use GPT-4o-mini for automated red team prompt generation — reserve GPT-4o for complex jailbreak analysis
Implement tiered moderation — fast keyword blocklist first, then Content Safety API only for non-blocked content
Cache safety decisions for identical prompts — reduces redundant API calls by 20-30%
Run red team sweeps on a schedule (nightly) rather than continuously — saves 60% on OpenAI costs
